title: We Can't Stop Thinking About This Golf Course in New Zealand
body:
We have played Old Head in Ireland and called it Pebble Beach on steroids. Cape Kidnappers looks like Old Head on steroids. We have not been yet and we are already planning the trip.

I lived in Auckland for two years and have been dying to get back ever since. I have never seen green as green as New Zealand. The whole country looks like it was painted. Cape Kidnappers is the reason we are finally making it happen.

Tom Doak designed it in 2004 on a 6,000 acre working sheep and cattle station on a peninsula extending 6.5 miles into the South Pacific. Golf Magazine ranks it 55th best in the world. The fairways sit 300 to 500 feet above the ocean on dramatic cliff edges with deep ravines running between them.

## The Course
### Cape Kidnappers
 
![Cape Kidnappers Back 9 (Golf Digest)](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1780515731/Golf_Digest_Photo_qjbrpb.webp)
*Cape Kidnappers Back 9 (Golf Digest)*

The course opens inland and builds anticipation through a front nine that is excellent in its own right before the cliffs reveal themselves on the back nine. Doak himself said it best, “Standing 140 meters above sea level looking out across the waves far below in Hawke's Bay is an overwhelming experience.”

The back nine follows fingers of land out toward the cliff edges and the demands on accuracy increase with every hole. Miss a shot in the wrong direction and the ball falls into a ravine or off a cliff entirely. The greens are fast with false fronts and the wind off the South Pacific changes your club selection on every single hole.

## The Hole
### Hole 15 - Pirate's Plank

A 650 yard par 5 that runs along a cliff finger with a 140 meter drop on the left and a 20 meter drop on the right. The fairway narrows the closer you get to the green. Four short and straight shots wins the hole. Go long over the green and your ball falls for 10 seconds before it hits the Pacific Ocean. That is not a metaphor, that is the actual yardage chart description of the hole.

![Hole 15](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1780515712/Hole_15_txy9qb.avif)
*Hole 15*

This is the most photographed hole in all of New Zealand.

## The Numbers
### You'll Get the American Discount

Green fees run approximately $250 to $500 USD depending on the season. The Rosewood Cape Kidnappers lodge on the property is considered one of the finest hotels in the world with 22 rooms, two restaurants, a spa, and 180 degree views of the Pacific. This is not a budget trip and it is not meant to be.

Auckland is your entry point with Napier about a four hour drive south or a quick domestic flight.

 [book-trip url="www.robertsonlodges.com/the-lodges/cape-kidnappers" title="Check it out!" cta="See Details"]
 
## Beyond the Golf
### Hawke's Bay is Worth the Trip
 
The ocean is warm enough to swim in from November through March with long stretches of sandy beach right outside your door. The mountain ranges behind the region rise to nearly 6,000 feet with snow on them in winter and helicopter tours that take you right to the top on the days between rounds. Te Mata Peak is the hike everyone does and the views from the summit look back across the vineyards and all the way out to the Pacific. After all of that Hawke's Bay has 72 vineyards and 30 cellar doors and the wine is world class.

One more thing. Get the lamb. There is no better place in the world to have it and that is not an exaggeration.

## The Insider
### Between October & May is Great

![Cape Kidnappers](https://res.cloudinary.com/dak67vton/image/upload/v1780515710/Additional_Check_it_Out_j4qjxg.jpg)
*Cape Kidnappers*

New Zealand's summer runs October through May which lines up perfectly with the Northern Hemisphere winter. Peak season rates apply during that window but so do the best conditions.

Tee times and the lodge book out well in advance for international visitors. Lock both in as soon as you decide you are going because this is not a trip you can plan last minute.

See you next Thursday.

Beau
excerpt: Cape Kidnappers Belongs On Every Golfer's Bucket List
author: Beau
